safest_plan.entry_basis: zero_live 2026-06-15 'Spot: 58, deeper 1-month breakout demand' and 2026-06-22 'accumulate physical lower toward ~58'; the green demand zone reads ~57.4-58.0 on both charts. This is a BUY (accumulate physical), not the short.
safest_plan.stop_basis: below the ~57.4 green demand / 1-month breakout demand zone that both charts mark as the accumulation floor; a close under it breaks the 'buy the flush' thesis

stance_by_source.summary: Three failed pushes at the ~70-71 ceiling led to continuation down; macro target ~58. For you, accumulate physical silver lower toward ~58, not the leveraged short.
2H chart: triple-top key level ~70-71, value-area-low ~68.4, continuation down toward the ~57.4-58.0 green demand zone, possibly after a retest higher first.
Accumulate spot at 58 after a closure into the deeper 1-month breakout demand.
Short was entered at the value-area-low ~67.77 but flagged as a touch early and haram; your spot angle is to accumulate physical lower after the flush.

conflicts: The zero_tg short entry of 67.77 (value-area-low) was itself flagged as 'a touch early' versus the live's triple-top plan, and price has since fallen below it to ~63-65, so that entry is stale for a fresh short.

Two angles run in parallel: the leveraged trade is a CFD SHORT (off-limits for you on faith grounds), while your action is to BUY physical lower - same 'silver goes lower' view, opposite instrument and action.
plain_english: Every source agrees silver is grinding lower after failing three times at the ~70-71 ceiling, and they all point to roughly 58 as the area to step in. The leveraged short Zero took is a contract-for-difference and is off-limits for you, so ignore it - your version is simply buying physical silver, and only once it flushes down toward 58. Price is currently around 63-65, still above that zone, so there is nothing to do yet. Wait for the drop toward 58 to accumulate, and drop the idea if silver instead closes below about 57.
